thinking.text:
The solution still has many OCR errors. The game mentions 262 moves but I only have 76 commands (filtered). The problem is that OCR captures partial commands as the player types them character by character. I need a better approach.

Let me think about this differently. Instead of trying to OCR each frame and track typing changes, I should look at the transcript in each frame. The transcript shows the history of completed commands. Each frame shows a portion of the game history. I need to look at frames throughout the video to capture all commands.

Let me examine the OCR output more carefully to understand what commands were actually typed. I'll look at the complete text from several frames to see the full transcript.

Actually, I think the best approach is to use the HQ frames (5fps = 951 frames) which have more temporal resolution. The 1fps set has too much time between frames, so commands get typed and completed between frames.

Let me extract frames at 10fps or even extract just the keyframes (scene changes) using ffmpeg's scene detection.

Or better yet, let me look at the full text of frames at key moments to understand the command sequence.

From the OCR outputs, I can see commands like:

- u (up)
- get
- d (down)
- s (south)

Let me look at the frames around where 'ulysse' appears, and 'Dopen', 'drpo', etc.
